title: Orchestration Policy Auditor
purpose: Find local/static orchestration policy regressions in repo docs, prompts, routine specs, routine reports, and ledger/event files with deterministic named rules, without mutating repository or orchestration state.
trigger: Run after changing orchestrator prompts, README/SKILL guidance, heartbeat rules, delegated worker contracts, or routine reports, especially after a real Codex App workflow exposes a repeatable orchestration mistake.
allowedActions: inspect repo-local docs, prompts, routine specs, routine reports, and ledger/event files read-only
scan for dry-run dispatch barrier violations
scan for main-checkout fallback implementation violations
scan for heartbeat continuation guard violations
scan for delegated worker boundary omissions
scan for local/proxy/weak evidence promotion to direct proof
write a RoutineRunReport JSON when requested
forbiddenActions: stage, commit, merge, push, tag, or release
dispatch sessions or start nested subagents
walk hidden user config outside the inspected repository
claim semantic proof of a violation from heuristic text matches
gates: repo-local orchestration policy input files collected
local report includes rule-hit summary counts when findings are present

evidence.direct: Reserved for future human-reviewed Codex App transcript proof; the MVP does not emit direct proof.
evidence.proxy: Reserved for accepted external review artifacts or transcript summaries; the MVP normally does not emit proxy proof.
evidence.local: Local static scan of repo-local orchestration docs, prompts, routine specs, routine reports, and ledger/event files with deterministic rule-hit summaries.
evidence.blocked: Repository or orchestration policy audit inputs cannot be inspected safely.

escalation: Treat findings as local/static suspicions until a reviewer confirms the prompt or docs issue.
Stop if repository inputs cannot be inspected.
Do not edit prompts, docs, or routine reports from this routine; report drift and let a separate implementation pass fix it.
